name: Castparts Employees
description_short: Federal credit union established in 1957 serving metal manufacturing employees in Portland, Oregon, with checking, savings, loans, and investment products.
description_long:
Castparts Employees Federal Credit Union was established in 1957 and is headquartered in Portland, Oregon. The institution operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution specifically chartered to serve employees of Castparts and related metal manufacturing industries. With over 65 years of operational history, CEFCU has built a focused membership base around a specific industry vertical rather than the general public.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business financial products including checking accounts (Share Draft accounts with 2,200+ members), savings accounts (4,900+ members), money market accounts, certificates of deposit (400+ members), credit cards, auto loans (new and used), mortgages (fixed-rate and adjustable-rate options), personal loans, and investment products like IRA/KEOGH accounts. Digital services include online banking, mobile app access for iOS and Android, bill pay, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and electronic signature authentication. The institution also provides financial counseling and financial education resources.

Castparts Employees FCU distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on a single industry sector—metal manufacturing—allowing it to tailor products and understand the unique economic needs of its membership. The institution stewards $76.3 million in assets and maintains a lean operational footprint with 1 physical branch located at 8120 SE Luther Road in Portland and 5 ATMs serving its 4,900+ loyal members. This specialized niche approach differs from larger regional or national credit unions serving broader populations.

For eligible metal manufacturing employees, CEFCU provides solid access to competitive rates and personalized service typical of smaller credit unions. However, the extremely limited branch network (1 location) and membership eligibility restrictions to specific industries significantly limit accessibility. As a small institution, it may lack the technological sophistication and product breadth of larger competitors, and online reviews are absent, making independent verification of service quality difficult. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Fixed-Rate Mortgages (15+ years and under 15 years)
Online Banking with bill pay and external transfers
pros: Member-owned cooperative structure with no profit motive, focusing on member benefits
Specialized focus on metal manufacturing industry with tailored financial products
$76.3 million in assets provides stability and NCUA insurance protection
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile app, remote deposit capture, and online bill pay
Offers mortgages with fixed-rate (15+ years and under 15 years) and adjustable-rate options
Low-cost checking via Share Draft accounts with 2,200+ active members
Financial counseling and education services provided to members
cons: Extremely limited physical presence with only 1 branch, requiring most members to use ATMs or digital banking
Restricted membership eligibility—only available to metal manufacturing industry employees, excluding general public
No online reviews available to assess member satisfaction or service quality independently
Small institution may lack advanced technology features and product variety of larger competitors
Lobby currently closed according to website, limiting in-person service access
best_for: Castparts and metal manufacturing company employees seeking industry-specific financial services
Members in Portland, Oregon area with digital banking comfort due to single-branch limitation
Individuals prioritizing member-owned cooperative structure and lower fees over branch convenience
Metal manufacturing workers seeking personal loans, auto financing, or mortgage products
diagnosis: Castparts Employees FCU is best for metal manufacturing workers in the Portland area seeking competitive rates and member-owned cooperative benefits, particularly those comfortable with digital banking given the single physical branch. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to specific industries, making it inaccessible to the general public, and the minimal branch footprint requires reliance on ATM and online services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
